_comment: sovereign-os four-watchdog (IPS spine) dashboard. Visualizes the selfdef-side selfdef_four_watchdog_* gauges shipped via the selfdef-four-watchdog-doctor.{service,timer} pair (selfdef commits 7869a45 + a009b39) into node_exporter's textfile_collector. Pairs with the 4 alert rules (FourWatchdogWorstSeverityCritical/AnyWarn/TextfileEmitFailed/ObserverSilent) in config/prometheus/alerts/four-watchdog.rules.yml and the runbook sections in docs/operator/m060-deployment-guide.md. The four-watchdog set is the IPS spine per SECURITY.md and SDD-004 (MS046 process / MS047 perimeter / MS044 tamper / MS048 config). Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Project boundary R10212: pure observability — no mutation surface.

panels.title: worst severity (0=ok 1=warn 2=critical)
panels.description: Single rollup: the worst severity across the 9 four-watchdog alert classifications (MS046 process / MS047 perimeter / MS044 tamper / MS048 config). Drives the FourWatchdogWorstSeverityCritical (>= 2) and FourWatchdogAnyWarn (== 1) alerts.

panels.title: observer age (s)
panels.description: Seconds since the selfdef-four-watchdog-doctor.timer last fired. Red at 300s (matches the FourWatchdogObserverSilent alert threshold + the M060 chain-stale threshold — locked by cross-surface threshold-lockstep contract).

panels.title: alerts at WARN+ (severity ≥1)
panels.description: How many of the 9 four-watchdog alert classifications are in WARN or CRITICAL right now. 0 = IPS spine green.

panels.title: observer emit-failed
panels.description: Honest-offline sentinel. 1 = the wrapper failed to emit fresh gauges (selfdefctl absent / jq absent / daemon unreachable / malformed JSON). When this is 1, do NOT trust the other 3 gauge series — they are STALE. Drives the FourWatchdogTextfileEmitFailed alert.

panels.title: per-alert severity over time
panels.description: One series per alert classification — labelled by MS-family (alert/ms/series). 0 = OK, 1 = WARN, 2 = CRITICAL. Lets operators see when a degradation started + which IPS subsystem broke. Group by `ms` label in Grafana legend for the milestone-family breakdown (MS046/MS047/MS044/MS048).

panels.title: worst-severity rollup over time
panels.description: Single rollup line tracking the worst case across all 9 alert classifications. The FourWatchdogWorstSeverityCritical alert fires when this >= 2 for 2m; FourWatchdogAnyWarn fires when this == 1 for 5m. The red threshold step at 2 matches the alert rule + the operator-readable severity ladder (0/1/2/-1).

panels.title: current per-alert state (drill-down)
panels.description: Live triage table. One row per alert classification with the milestone-family (ms) + per-series labels. State column maps severity numeric → operator-readable label (OK/WARN/CRITICAL/UNKNOWN). Sort by severity descending to surface the firing alerts at the top during incident response.

panels.title: observer-freshness — emit-failed sentinel over time
panels.description: The honest-offline sentinel gauge. Red bands mark wedged-wrapper windows when the operator MUST NOT trust the other 3 gauge series. Pairs with the FourWatchdogTextfileEmitFailed alert (5m for-window).

panels.title: observer-age timeseries (alert threshold at 300s)
panels.description: Seconds since the doctor timer last fired, plotted over time. Red threshold at 300s matches the FourWatchdogObserverSilent alert + the M060 chain-stale threshold — locked by cross-surface threshold-lockstep contract test.

title: sovereign-os — four-watchdog (IPS spine)
